task.description: Add json output flag to mycel maintain

preconditions: branch feat/json-maintain is checked out from main with a clean working tree
crates/mycel-cli/src/main.rs compiles with no errors on the current HEAD
the maintain subcommand is registered in the clap App definition at line ~180 of crates/mycel-cli/src/main.rs
success_criteria: `cargo test -p mycel-cli` passes with exit code 0 and the json-maintain test fixture reports structured JSON
crates/mycel-cli/src/main.rs contains a --json flag wired to the maintain subcommand output path
running `mycel maintain --json` emits a valid JSON object to stdout instead of plain text

inherited_context.claim: maintain subcommand already exists and is routed through the CLI dispatch table
clap v4 is the arg-parser crate in use; v3 API is gone
the json output format for other subcommands uses serde_json::to_string and prints to stdout

refusal_risks: do not change the default (non-json) output format — existing callers pipe the plain-text output
do not bump the CLI major version in this PR

preconditions: the failing test `distill_truncation_boundary` exists in crates/mycel-core/src/lib.rs and currently panics
crates/mycel-core/src/lib.rs at the truncation call site uses a byte-index slice that is known to cut a multi-byte char
the test corpus fixture at crates/mycel-tests/tests/fixtures/decay_cases.jsonl is intact and unchanged
success_criteria: `cargo test -p mycel-core distill_truncation_boundary` passes with exit code 0
the fix uses `char_indices` or `chars().take(n)` so no multi-byte char is split
crates/mycel-core/src/lib.rs diff shows removal of the raw byte-index slice on the truncation path

inherited_context.claim: the off-by-one was first observed when a 3-byte UTF-8 emoji sat at exactly the truncation boundary
distill truncation limit is configurable via DistillConfig.max_chars but defaults to 512

refusal_risks: do not change the default truncation limit — callers depend on 512 chars
do not touch the decay scoring logic in the same commit

preconditions: crates/mycel-core/src/lib.rs contains the runs table DDL with an expires_at INTEGER column
the current schema user_version is 4 as confirmed by PRAGMA user_version
no migration for idx_runs_expires_at exists in the existing DDL — grep shows zero results
success_criteria: `cargo test --workspace` passes with exit code 0 after adding the index DDL
the index statement `CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_runs_expires_at ON runs(expires_at)` is present in crates/mycel-core/src/lib.rs
PRAGMA user_version remains 4 — this is additive DDL, no schema bump

inherited_context.claim: the runs table has an expires_at column used by the decay model for TTL checks
all existing indexes follow the IF NOT EXISTS pattern so DDL is idempotent
a full table scan on runs.expires_at was flagged as slow in the v0.2 performance audit

refusal_risks: do not bump user_version — the index is additive and no migration number is needed
do not add a composite index without a separate ADR decision

preconditions: crates/mycel-cli/src/main.rs exists with the top-level clap App and at least one registered subcommand
the Db::open and substrate runtime are importable from mycel-core in the CLI crate
no list-runs subcommand exists — grep of crates/mycel-cli/src/main.rs returns zero matches for "list-runs" or "list_runs"
success_criteria: `cargo test -p mycel-cli` passes with exit code 0 and includes a list_runs_subcommand_registered test
running `mycel list-runs` outputs one run per line with fields id, created_at, and status in tabular form
crates/mycel-cli/src/main.rs contains a match arm for the "list-runs" subcommand that calls the substrate query

inherited_context.claim: the CLI uses a match on subcommand name string to dispatch — no macro routing
Substrate::runs_since is the query method to use; it returns Vec<Run> ordered by created_at desc
other list commands (list-antibodies) format output as tab-separated columns

refusal_risks: do not add pagination in this PR — scope is a simple unfiltered list
do not open a persistent Db connection in the CLI — open and close per invocation

task.description: Add count field to maintenance audit payload

preconditions: crates/mycel-core/src/lib.rs contains the MaintenanceAudit struct with at least pruned_count and decay_applied fields
the audit payload is serialized to JSON via serde and stored in the audit_log table
no count field exists in MaintenanceAudit — grep confirms zero matches for "total_count" or "run_count"
success_criteria: `cargo test -p mycel-core` passes with exit code 0 with no regressions to existing audit tests
MaintenanceAudit in crates/mycel-core/src/lib.rs contains a pub run_count: usize field
the JSON output of a maintenance audit contains a "run_count" key when deserialized by serde_json

inherited_context.claim: MaintenanceAudit is serialized with serde_json::to_string and the result stored in audit_log.payload
adding a field with serde default is backwards-compatible with existing audit log rows
the v0.3 roadmap calls for richer audit payloads to support the v0.4 projection engine

refusal_risks: do not rename existing fields — the audit log JSON is read by external tooling
do not add a database column — run_count is computed at maintenance time and stored in the JSON blob only

task.description: Wire refusal risk logging to sentinel audit trail

preconditions: crates/mycel-core/src/lib.rs contains the SentinelAudit struct and the sentinel_audit table DDL
the refusal_risks field of SelfSpec is populated when a spec is inserted via SpecStore::insert
grep of crates/mycel-core/src/lib.rs shows zero calls to insert refusal_risks into sentinel_audit
success_criteria: `cargo test -p mycel-core sentinel_refusal_logged` passes with exit code 0
inserting a spec with 2 refusal risks via SpecStore::insert results in 2 rows in sentinel_audit with source = spec:<signature>
crates/mycel-core/src/selfspec.rs diff shows SpecStore::insert calling sentinel_audit_write for each refusal risk

inherited_context.claim: sentinel_audit_write inserts a row with fields (id, rule_sig, matched_text, action, created_at)
refusal risks are free-text strings; the rule_sig field should be set to the spec signature for traceability
SpecStore::insert already holds a rusqlite transaction so the sentinel writes can share it

refusal_risks: do not modify the sentinel_audit table DDL — no new columns
do not roll back the spec insert if a sentinel write fails — log the error and continue

preconditions: PRAGMA user_version is currently 4 as confirmed by `sqlite3 ~/.mycel/mycel.db PRAGMA user_version`
crates/mycel-core/src/lib.rs contains the migration ladder with explicit version checks
the v5 migration adds the spec_tags table — no such table exists in the current schema
success_criteria: `cargo test --workspace` passes with exit code 0 and open_in_memory_user_version_stays_5 asserts version 5
PRAGMA user_version returns 5 after the migration runs on a fresh in-memory database
the spec_tags table DDL is present in crates/mycel-core/src/lib.rs with columns id, spec_sig, tag, created_at

inherited_context.claim: the migration ladder pattern in lib.rs applies migrations sequentially by comparing user_version
each migration is wrapped in a transaction so a partial failure leaves the database unchanged
spec_tags was approved in the v0.3 planning session as the tagging primitive for the v0.4 search index

refusal_risks: do not modify the v4 migration path — existing databases must upgrade cleanly
do not add spec_tags to SpecStore in this PR — DDL only, the query layer comes in the next task

task.description: Add cargo test step to CI workflow

preconditions: .github/workflows/ci.yml exists and currently runs cargo build but has no cargo test step
the workspace Cargo.toml at /Volumes/T7/Mycel/Cargo.toml lists all member crates
the CI runner uses ubuntu-latest with the stable Rust toolchain pinned to 1.78.0
success_criteria: the CI workflow file .github/workflows/ci.yml contains a step with `run: cargo test --workspace` after the build step
a push to feat/substrate-ecology-v02-v05 triggers the CI run and the test step exits with code 0
grep of .github/workflows/ci.yml returns at least 1 match for "cargo test --workspace"

inherited_context.claim: the CI workflow already caches the cargo registry and target directory via actions/cache
the MYCEL_DB_PATH env var must be set to a temp path in CI or tests that open a real DB will fail
the mycel-tests integration tests require CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR which cargo test sets automatically

refusal_risks: do not pin the actions/checkout or rust-toolchain actions to a new SHA without reviewing the changelog
do not add a separate lint step in this PR — clippy is scoped to a follow-up task

task.description: Emit structured event on spec insertion

preconditions: crates/mycel-core/src/selfspec.rs contains SpecStore::insert which currently writes only to the specs table
an event bus or callback mechanism exists in crates/mycel-core/src/lib.rs — confirmed by grep for EventBus or on_event
the event type for spec insertion is not yet defined — grep shows zero matches for SpecInserted
success_criteria: `cargo test -p mycel-core spec_insert_emits_event` passes with exit code 0
SpecStore::insert calls the event bus with a SpecInserted { signature, id, created_at } payload after a successful write
crates/mycel-core/src/selfspec.rs diff shows the SpecInserted variant added to the Event enum

inherited_context.claim: the Event enum is defined in crates/mycel-core/src/lib.rs and is serializable via serde
EventBus::publish takes an Event and a unix timestamp; it is infallible and logs errors internally
the v0.4 projection engine subscribes to SpecInserted to build the spec count projection

refusal_risks: do not make SpecStore::insert fail if event publication fails — the write is authoritative
do not add persistence for events in this PR — the bus is in-memory only in v0.3

task.description: Fix panic on empty inherited context in spec store list

preconditions: a repro test `spec_store_list_panics_on_empty_context` exists in crates/mycel-core/src/selfspec.rs and currently panics at the unwrap on line ~230
SpecStore::list in crates/mycel-core/src/selfspec.rs calls serde_json::from_str and unwraps the result with map_err(to_sql_error)
the panic reproduces on the current HEAD with `cargo test -p mycel-core spec_store_list_panics_on_empty_context 2>&1 | grep FAILED`
success_criteria: `cargo test -p mycel-core` passes with exit code 0 with no panics
SpecStore::list returns Err(MycelError::InvalidSpec(...)) instead of panicking when a stored JSON row is malformed
the fix is in crates/mycel-core/src/selfspec.rs and uses ? propagation instead of unwrap or expect

inherited_context.claim: to_sql_error converts a serde_json::Error to a rusqlite::Error for propagation through the conn.query_map pipeline
the panic was introduced in commit b6997fa when the spec_json column was added without a NOT NULL constraint on inner arrays
serde_json::from_str returns Err for a JSON object missing required fields like inherited_context

refusal_risks: do not swallow the error silently — return Err so the caller can handle or log it
do not change the spec_json column type or add a NOT NULL constraint in this fix — schema changes need a migration

task.description: Add dry-run flag to mycel distill

preconditions: crates/mycel-cli/src/main.rs contains the distill subcommand and dispatches to mycel_core::distill
the distill function in crates/mycel-core/src/lib.rs writes decay scores back to the substrate on each invocation
no dry_run parameter exists in DistillConfig — grep of crates/mycel-core/src/lib.rs returns zero matches
success_criteria: `cargo test -p mycel-cli distill_dry_run_no_writes` passes with exit code 0
running `mycel distill --dry-run` prints the would-be decay scores to stdout but makes zero writes to the substrate database
crates/mycel-core/src/lib.rs diff shows DistillConfig gains a pub dry_run: bool field defaulting to false

inherited_context.claim: DistillConfig is constructed in the CLI and passed directly to distill() — the CLI owns config construction
the substrate write in distill is a single call to Substrate::apply_decay_batch with a Vec<DecayUpdate>
dry-run is a standard pattern in other Mycel subcommands — maintain already has it

refusal_risks: do not skip the decay score computation in dry-run mode — only skip the write
do not add a --dry-run flag to any subcommand other than distill in this PR

task.description: Add signature uniqueness check before spec insert

preconditions: SpecStore::insert in crates/mycel-core/src/selfspec.rs currently allows duplicate signatures — grep shows no SELECT before INSERT
the specs table has an index on signature (idx_specs_signature) so a SELECT query is cheap
a test `spec_store_rejects_duplicate_signature` does not yet exist — grep returns zero matches
success_criteria: `cargo test -p mycel-core spec_store_rejects_duplicate_signature` passes with exit code 0
SpecStore::insert returns Err(MycelError::InvalidSpec("duplicate_signature")) when a spec with the same signature already exists in the table
crates/mycel-core/src/selfspec.rs diff shows a SELECT COUNT(*) check before the INSERT in SpecStore::insert

inherited_context.claim: the specs table uses TEXT PRIMARY KEY on id (uuid) not on signature, so the DB does not enforce signature uniqueness
dedupe_specs collapses duplicates at import time but SpecStore::insert has no such guard
returning a typed error rather than relying on a UNIQUE constraint keeps the error surface consistent with validate()

refusal_risks: do not add a UNIQUE constraint to the specs table — that is a schema change requiring a migration
do not check uniqueness after the INSERT — check before so no partial state is written
